chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْبِدَعِ وَ الرَّأْيِ وَ الْمَقَايِيسِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – The innovation, and the opinion, and the analogies

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (198/ 1)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (198/ 1)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقِيَاسِ فَقَالَ مَا لَكُمْ وَ الْقِيَاسَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُسْأَلُ كَيْفَ أَحَلَّ وَ كَيْفَ حَرَّمَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan Musa<sup>asws</sup> about the analogy, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘What is it to you all and the analogy? Allah<sup>azwj</sup> will not Ask how it was Made to be Permissible and how it was Made to be Prohibited?’.
en.sarwar: 16. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa who has said the following. “I asked (Imam) abu al-Hassan Musa (a.s.) about the use of analogy and the Imam said, ‘What do you have to do with analogy? Allah will not ask how it was made lawful or unlawful.’”
